name: Bill Watrous
abstract: William Russell Watrous III (June 8, 1939 – July 2, 2018) was an American jazz trombonist. He is perhaps best known for his rendition of Sammy Nestico's arrangement of the Johnny Mandel ballad "A Time for Love", which he recorded on a 1993 album of the same name.

interviews.title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Bill Watrous only in response to the interviewer's comparison of their playing styles; no personal interaction or direct relationship is described by the interviewee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, yeah. We'll put a name to him in a minute. (Bill Watrous -- ed.)

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Did you play with Watrous and Rosolino (unintelligible) this year at Dick Gibson's? [INTERVIEWEE] Not this year. Last year I did. Al Grey played with them this year. But last year I played with them.

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: he hired me... I orchestrated an album... heard the orchestrations I did with Bill... hopefully Bill Watrous and I will be doing another album soon.
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate to work with Bill Watrous myself quite a bit... when we were recording the “Absolute Trombone” record
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten to play with some of the greatest man. I mean that CD we did together... Bill Watrous
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]
